channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Trump signs executive order to end collective bargaining at agencies involved with national security
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump moved Thursday to end collective bargaining with federal labor unions in agencies with national security missions across the federal government, citing authority granted him under a 1978 law.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Stanford, Cal and UCLA investigated in Trump's anti-DEI campaign
item.description: The Trump administration has opened investigations into the admissions policies at Stanford University and three campuses within the University of California system, including UC Berkeley, UCLA and UC Irvine, the Department of Justice said Thursday. U.S.
item.dc:creator: JOCELYN GECKER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Turkish student at Tufts University is latest Palestinian supporter swept up in US crackdown
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — A Turkish student detained by federal officers as she walked along a street in a Boston suburb is the latest supporter of Palestinian causes to be swept up in the Trump administration’s crackdown on immigrants who express their political views.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, JAKE OFFENHARTZ and KATHY McCORMACK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: NY county clerk refuses to file Texas' fine for doctor accused of prescribing abortion pills
item.description: A county clerk in New York refused Thursday to file a more than $100,000 judgment from Texas against a doctor accused of prescribing abortion pills to a woman near Dallas, setting up a potential challenge to laws designed to shield abortion providers who serve patients in states with abortion bans.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL HILL, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Judge: High heat in Texas prisons unconstitutional
item.description: A federal judge found the extreme heat in Texas prisons is “plainly unconstitutional” but did not order the state to begin installing air conditioners.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KRON4

item.title: Bagpiper dies doing popular vacation attraction days before missing son’s remains found in backyard treehouse
item.description: A Georgia bagpiper died in a scuba diving accident in Hawaii, less than a week before his missing son's skeletal remains were found in a backyard treehouse of his previous home.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Florida considers bill to roll back child labor laws to fill jobs once held by undocumented migrants
item.description: Florida lawmakers are considering major changes to state child labor laws to address a potential shortage of migrant workers due to GOP-led immigration crackdowns, which would allow teens to work longer hours and earn lower wages.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Judge says extreme heat in Texas prisons is unconstitutional but doesn't order they install AC
item.description: AUSTIN, Texas (AP) — A federal judge on Wednesday found the extreme heat in Texas prisons is “plainly unconstitutional,” but declined to order the state to immediately start installing air conditioning, which could cost billions.
item.dc:creator: JIM VERTUNO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Ex-North Dakota lawmaker gets 10 years for going to Europe with intent to pay for sex with a minor
item.description: A once-powerful former North Dakota lawmaker was sentenced Wednesday to 10 years in prison for traveling to Europe with the intent to pay for sex with a minor.
item.dc:creator: JACK DURA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: A heart-shaped note was found in socks bound for Luigi Mangione
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Someone tucked a heart-shaped note of encouragement into socks packed for Luigi Mangione to wear to court last month in the UnitedHealthcare CEO murder case, according to court documents made public Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Stepmother accused of holding man captive for over 20 years appears in court
item.description: Kimberly Sullivan arrived at Waterbury Superior Court just before 10 a.m., where she was expected to plead not guilty to kidnapping and assault charges, according to her lawyers. But the case was continued until Friday.
item.dc:creator: Jenn Brink, KTLA 5

item.title: Justice Department goes to bat for Trump in hush money case
item.description: The Justice Department went to bat for President Trump in his hush money criminal case Wednesday, urging for the case to be moved so he can attempt to toss his guilty verdict on presidential immunity grounds.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, Fox 5

item.title: Chewing gum releases thousands of microplastics into body, study finds
item.description: A recent study found that chewing gum can release up to thousands of microplastics per piece and potentially be ingested, according to researchers.
item.dc:creator: Rhea Caoile, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Kansas measles cases double to 23 and new Ohio outbreak sickens 10
item.description: A measles outbreak in Kansas doubled in less than a week to 23 cases and has "a possible link” to outbreaks in Texas and New Mexico that have sickened more than 370, the state health department said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: DEVI SHASTRI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: San Diego passes first-in-the-nation policy to ban digital-only coupons at grocery stores
item.description: The San Diego City Council has passed the Grocery Pricing Transparency Ordinance, requiring grocers to offer both digital and paper coupons to all customers, promoting equal access to grocery savings opportunities.
item.dc:creator: Anna Ashcraft, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Doctor accused of trying to kill his wife during hike
item.description: Police say the man tried to push his wife off the trail and allegedly hit her in the head multiple times with a rock.
item.dc:creator: Jill Kuramoto, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Sewage sludge can find a second life on farm fields. Here's how it's made
item.description: What goes down your toilet can end up on farm fields across the United States. Biosolids, or sewage sludge, are the solid byproducts of the wastewater treatment process. Rich in nutrients, they can be used as fertilizer on agricultural fields or compost on lawns.
item.dc:creator: JOSHUA A. BICKEL, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: How the Signal 'group chat' furor has affected the 5 biggest players
item.description: The fact that such inherently sensitive information was being communicated in such a forum — and in front of a reporter whom no other participant appears to have noticed — has caused a political storm.
item.dc:creator: Niall Stanage, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Pilot and 2 young daughters survive the night on airplane wing after crashing into icy Alaska lake
item.description: ANCHORAGE, Alaska (AP) — A pilot and his two young daughters survived on the wing of a plane for about 12 hours after it crashed and was partially submerged in an icy Alaska lake, then were rescued after being spotted by a good Samaritan.
item.dc:creator: MARK THIESSEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Oscar-winning Palestinian director says Israeli soldiers beat him after attack by settlers
item.description: HEBRON, West Bank (AP) — Only a few weeks ago, Hamdan Ballal stood on a stage in Los Angeles accepting an Oscar for the film “No Other Land,” a documentary depicting his West Bank village’s struggle against Israel’s occupation.
item.dc:creator: JULIA FRANKEL, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Barely into spring, Phoenix flirts with first triple-digit heat day of 2025
item.description: PHOENIX (AP) — Phoenix flirted with its first 100-degree day of the year on Tuesday, though it fell just short of that milestone. The metro area was expected to reach 100 degrees Fahrenheit (37.7 Celsius) on Tuesday, but the forecasted high temperature was later revised to 98 degrees (36.6 Celsius).
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump signs order seeking to overhaul US elections, including requiring proof of citizenship
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — President Donald Trump on Tuesday signed a sweeping executive action to overhaul elections in the U.S., including requiring documentary proof of citizenship to register to vote in federal elections and demanding that all ballots be received by Election Day. The order says the U.S.
item.dc:creator: ALI SWENSON and CHRISTINA A. CASSIDY, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: The MLB teams with the highest
item.description: A California team has the highest median ticket price, a new report found, but they don't play in Los Angeles.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, Fox 5

item.title: Senate confirms Trump's nominee to run NIH
item.description: The Senate on Tuesday confirmed health researcher Jay Bhattacharya as the next leader of the National Institutes of Health.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, Fox 5

item.title: House Democrats demand answers from Trump team about war plans Signal chat
item.description: The top Democrats on the House’s Defense, Oversight, Foreign Affairs, and Intelligence committees want answers from President Trump’s national security team, saying they’ve become “deeply troubled” after a journalist was unknowingly added to a group chat of senior officials discussing war plans.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, WGN 9

item.title: Judge orders a June trial for US government's felony case against Boeing
item.description: A federal judge in Texas has set a June trial date for the U.S. government's years-old conspiracy case against Boeing for misleading regulators about the 737 Max jetliner before two of the planes crashed, killing 346 people. U.S.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Judge temporarily blocks Trump admin from deporting South Korean Columbia student
item.description: A federal judge Tuesday blocked the Trump administration from deporting a Columbia University student who participated in pro-Palestinian demonstrations at the school.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, WGN 9

item.title: Family of Riley Strain files wrongful death lawsuit against Delta Chi fraternity
item.description: Family of Riley Strain, a University of Missouri student who died last year during a trip to Nashville, have filed a wrongful death lawsuit against his former fraternity and pledge brothers.
item.dc:creator: Joey Schneider, Fox 5

item.title: President Trump pardons former Hunter Biden business partner Devon Archer
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump on Tuesday pardoned a former business partner of Hunter Biden who was convicted of participating in a conspiracy to defraud a Native American tribe.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: More states move to ban cellphones in school as Georgia is latest to act
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — More states are moving to ban or restrict cellphones in school, a trend that continues to win bipartisan support in legislatures.
item.dc:creator: JEFF AMY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: The US Postal Service has been struggling for years. Now Trump's talking about privatizing it
item.description: The U.S. Postal Service is facing an uncertain future after the resignation this week of Postmaster General Louis DeJoy and the suggestion by President Donald Trump and Elon Musk, who heads the Department of Government Efficiency, that the mail service could be privatized.
item.dc:creator: SUSAN HAIGH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Tennessee pitcher Karlyn Pickens throws fastest pitch in college softball history
item.description: Tennessee softball standout Karlyn Pickens wrote her name into the history books on Monday with a record-breaking pitch.
item.dc:creator: Gregory Raucoules, WGN 9

item.title: Illinois returns stolen land to Prairie Band Potawatomi Nation
item.description: SPRINGFIELD, Ill. (AP) — The Prairie Land Potawatomi Nation has reclaimed land in Illinois that was promised to the tribe's leader 175 years ago but stolen by the federal government 20 years later. A law signed by Gov.
item.dc:creator: JOHN O'CONNOR, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump signs order targeting law firm that employed Mueller team prosecutor
item.description: President Trump signed an order Tuesday targeting a law firm that previously employed Andrew Weissmann, a former federal prosecutor who worked on then-special counsel Robert Mueller’s team.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Tax season is a prime time for scams. IRS uncertainty could add to the issues this year
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — There's a lot of information (and money) on the table during tax season. That also makes it a prime time for scams. Year-round, fraudsters may use a handful of common tactics to try to steal your identity, money or other sensitive information.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Suspect in Cal Fire captain's death arrested in Mexico: Police
item.description: Authorities previously said they believed Yolanda Olejniczak Marodi had fled to Mexico shortly after the alleged stabbing.
item.dc:creator: Anna Ashcraft, ABC 4

item.title: Young Michigan hockey player goes home after months of rehab
item.description: Months after a young hockey player checked in at Mary Free Bed Rehabilitation Hospital, it's safe to say he earned his tap-out.
item.dc:creator: Brittany Flowers, WGN 9
